summary: Smoky Wheel jam, rare Let It Grow>China>Rider and fiery Shakedown Street
blurb: The show bursts with a soaring Let It Grow → China Cat → I Know You Rider sequence and a monster Wheel jam that rolls into a legendary Shakedown Street. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, Brent supplies jazzy keyboards, and the drums/space segment tops any 1981 outing.
review: Long Beach ’81 stands out as one of the band’s high‑energy summer peaks. The first set builds to an unforgettable Let It Grow → China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider closing, where Jerry Garcia’s “shot‑out‑of‑a‑cannon” vibrola tone and lyrical phrasing lift the whole room. The second set is a non‑stop jam marathon: a pre‑drums Wheel that segues into NTAW, an extended Shakedown Street groove, and a Spanish Jam that reviewers call the best of any 1981 show. Brent Mydland shines with jazzy, Herbie‑Hancock‑esque keyboards and a fiery solo on Big River, while Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann lock in tight, propulsive rhythms throughout. Though a few mixes cut the intro to Jack Straw or mute some vocals, the overall performance is consistently praised as “blazing,” “legendary,” and “top‑notch.” The best listening experience comes from the Miller soundboard capture, which balances clarity with the band’s raw power.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six detailed recordings are overwhelmingly positive, repeatedly calling the show "legendary," "blazing," and "top‑notch." Multiple sources highlight the same standout moments (Let It Grow>China>Rider, Wheel/NTAW, Shakedown Street) and praise Jerry’s guitar, Brent’s keyboards, and the drum/space segment. No consistent negative commentary on performance quality was found.

best_recording.reason: High‑quality soundboard with clear, well‑balanced mix; captures the full set without major cuts and is praised as an outstanding 1981 SBD recording.

key_highlights: Rare Let It Grow → China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider sequence
Monstrous Wheel jam that flows into NTAW
Spanish Jam/drums/space segment deemed the best of any 1981 show

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers soaring vibrola tones, a "shot out of a cannon" solo on The Wheel and lyrical, swirling guitar work throughout the Let It Grow → China → Rider sequence.
band_performance.Bob: Weir provides solid rhythmic support and memorable whistle intro on Truckin', though his vocals are occasionally noted as muffled or sloppy.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland shines with jazzy, Herbie‑Hancock‑style keyboards, a standout solo on Big River, and tight interplay that drives the Shakedown Street groove.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming adds propulsive power to the Spanish Jam and space sections, contributing to what reviewers call the best drum/space segment of 1981.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann locks in with Hart, delivering a tight, energetic foundation that underpins the extended jams and keeps the groove relentless.
